title: Geum obliquum
taxonomy: Geum obliquum belongs to the family Rosaceae, which includes other plants such as roses and strawberries. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its botanical features. Geum obliquum is also a member of the genus Geum, which comprises several species of flowering plants. Within the genus, it is often classified as Geum obliquum (L.) DC, with the species name often attributed to the botanists Linnaeus and de Candolle. The plant's classification is essential for understanding its evolution, ecology, and conservation.
uses: Geum obliquum, also known as the Wood Avens, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's roots and leaves are said to have been used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and wounds. In folk medicine, it was used to treat ulcers and gum infections, as well as to reduce fever and alleviate digestive issues. Some studies suggest that Geum obliquum may have antioxidant properties, which could contribute to its potential health benefits. Further research is needed to confirm the plant's medicinal uses and potential applications.

side_effects: Geum obliquum, also known as the round-leaved avens, is not typically associated with side effects due to its limited use in herbal medicine. intro: Geum obliquum, also known as the water avens or alpine avens, is a perennial plant species in the family Rosaceae, characterized by its yellow flowers and distinctive leaf arrangement. The health benefits of Geum obliquum include its potential to support immune function and reduce inflammation, making it a popular herbal remedy for various conditions such as colds and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Geum obliquum include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Geum obliquum include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote relaxation, soothe digestive issues, and support overall well-being.
